source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260213 태국 MD Healthcard Co., Ltd/251209 보완서류/2.2 [Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector] Technical Document/A-CSDT-23G attachment (Rev.5)/9. Validation and verification/9.6 clinical/9.6.1 CEP/Clinical Evaluation Plan (rev.2).pdf

document.title: Clinical Evaluation Plan for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

sourceSummary.oneLine: Clinical Evaluation Plan Rev. 2 for H&abyz Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW under EU MDR clinical evaluation requirements.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports clinical evaluation planning, GSPR clinical evidence, benefit-risk assessment, equivalence justification, PMS/PMCF inputs, and standards mapping for MDR technical documentation.
keyPoints: The plan is documented according to Part A of Annex XIV of Regulation (EU) 2017/745.
The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy in adults and children, excluding infants and pregnant women.
Contraindications state the device is not for mammography, fluoroscopy, angiography, infants, or pregnant women.
Clinical benefits are reduction in patient dose with high quality image and fast image acquisition time.
Clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, recovery time, and image acquisition time.
The benchmark device is ADD, described as a previous generation device currently available for sale on the European market.
The document states there is no common specification for the device.
The clinical evaluation report update frequency is every year.

evidence.claim: The device is intended for general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.

evidence.claim: The device is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems.
evidence.evidenceText: It is intended to replace film based radiographic diagnostic systems

evidence.claim: The intended target groups are adults and children except infants and pregnant women.
evidence.evidenceText: Adult and children except infant and pregnant women.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector target population

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: Clinical benefits include reduction in patient dose with high quality image and fast image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: Clinical benefit 1 – Reduction in patient dose and high quality image. Clinical benefit 2 – Fast image acquisition time
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector clinical benefits

evidence.claim: The clinical outcome parameters include DQE, MTF, recovery time, and image acquisition time.
evidence.evidenceText: The relevant clinical outcome parameter ... is the detective quantum efficiency (DQE), modulation transfer function (MTF). For the second clinical outcome parameters are recovery time and image confirmation time.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector clinical outcome parameters

evidence.claim: Performance objectives are measured and tested according to EN 62220-1-1.
evidence.evidenceText: will be measured and tested in accordance with EN 62220-1-1.

evidence.claim: The document states the device has no components including pharmaceutical, non-viable animal, or human tissues.
evidence.evidenceText: doesn’t have components which include pharmaceutical, non-viable animal or human tissues.

evidence.claim: The benchmark device is ADD, a previous generation device available on the European market.
evidence.evidenceText: The benchmark device, which is previous generations of the device ... is ADD. The ADD is currently available for sale on European market.

evidence.claim: The proposed devices are detectors with indirect energy conversion.
evidence.evidenceText: These proposed devices are the detectors with indirect energy conversion.

evidence.claim: The device is not subject to clinical investigation according to the required level of clinical evidence section.
evidence.evidenceText: Our device is not subject to Clinical investigation.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector clinical investigation

evidence.claim: PMS data are collected to evaluate undesirable side effects, benefit-risk acceptability, and GSPR conformity over the device lifetime.
evidence.evidenceText: PMS data ... is collected according to the PMS plan to evaluate the undesirable side effects and the acceptability of the benefit-risk ratio and to confirm the conformity of the relevant general safety and performance over the entire lifetime.
evidence.pageTarget: Post-market surveillance for Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The PMCF will be conducted as part of PMS activities.
evidence.evidenceText: The PMCF will be conducted as a part of the PMS activities.

evidence.claim: The document states there is no common specification for the device.
evidence.evidenceText: There is no common specification for the device.

evidence.claim: The clinical evaluation report is updated every year.
evidence.evidenceText: The frequency of updates for the clinical evaluation report is every year.

reviewFlags.reason: The source is an internal confidential technical documentation extract, not a regulator-issued certificate or public authority decision.
Notified Body 3EC is referenced in revision history, but no certificate number, formal conformity assessment decision, or current CE certificate status is provided.
Some source text contains minor OCR or drafting errors, but key metadata and regulatory facts are readable.
